page: Amazon Web Services c8i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture, offering 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8i.xlarge demonstrates strong single-core performance, achieving top-tier results in Geekbench single-core testing and text processing. Single-threaded operations like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top tier. However, multi-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng multi-core and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falling into the lower performance tier. Memory performance is mixed; while large-block uncached read and write bandwidth tests show average throughput, small-block cached read bandwidth and Passmark memory scores are relatively weak.

The hardware configuration presents a clear tradeoff, offering high single-threaded compute capability and advanced instruction sets, such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX for matrix operations, but limited memory capacity at 8.0 GB. This resource density makes it a cost-efficient option for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The c8i.xlarge is well-suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, web serving, and lightweight compilation tasks, but is less optimal for memory-intensive databases or highly parallelized multi-core processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 4 dedicated vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache and 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks highlight exceptional single-core and text-processing capabilities, alongside strong single-threaded compression performance, though multi-core and cached memory bandwidth results are weaker. With advanced instruction support like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, the c8i.xlarge represents a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy, single-threaded tasks, text processing, and web serving, while its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it less suitable for memory-intensive workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8i.xlarge is an Intel-powered, compute-optimized instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and DDR5 memory for single-threaded and text-processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (2.0 GB per core)
Strong single-core and single-threaded compression benchmark performance
Support for advanced vector and matrix instruction sets including AVX-512 and AMX
Best suited for text processing, web serving, and single-threaded compute tasks
